name: Architext 1.2.0 Release Truth
summary: Release Truth makes release posture, scope, blockers, milestones, and historical trends first-class Architext data.

required.title: Release index and detail schema contract
required.summary: Define optional manifest-driven release data without invalidating existing repositories.

required.title: Release validation
required.summary: Validate release index/detail files and broken release references through the existing validate lifecycle.

required.title: Native Release Truth mode
required.summary: Add a top-level Release Truth viewer mode for current release posture and history.

required.title: Historical release trends
required.summary: Render a compact history view with feature and bug-fix count trends by release date.

planned.title: Architext self release fixture
planned.summary: Describe the latest shipped Release Truth release in Architext's own data.

planned.title: Doctor reporting for release data
planned.summary: Report stale release metadata, broken dependencies, and count mismatches through doctor.

stretch.title: Additional trend chart series
stretch.summary: Add optional blocker, scope-size, and stretch-item trend lines after the first chart is proven.
stretch.rationale: Additional chart series beyond features and bug fixes remain optional after the first Release Truth chart shipped.

stretch.title: Strict detail-on-demand loading
stretch.summary: Optimize historical detail loading once the first UI proves the release contract.

outOfScope.title: Network release ingestion
outOfScope.summary: Release Truth must not depend on GitHub or npm network access for the initial release.

workstreams.name: Release data contract
workstreams.summary: Define index/detail files, schema vocabulary, and self-hosted fixture data.

workstreams.name: Lifecycle validation
workstreams.summary: Use existing validate and doctor commands for release data correctness.

workstreams.name: Release Truth viewer
workstreams.summary: Add the current release snapshot, workstreams, blockers, milestones, and history trend UI.

workstreams.name: Historical loading performance
workstreams.summary: Keep history navigation cheap by loading summaries first and details only when needed.

dependencies.summary: Validation must use the finalized release schema vocabulary.
The viewer should render the same release data contract used by validation.
History navigation depends on the native Release Truth mode.
